title: India, France adopt Innovation Roadmap 2030, Economic Security Dialogue
content: At bilateral meeting in French city of Nice, Prime Minister Modi and French President Macron call for fast adoption of the India-EU FTA, agree to create Joint India-France AI Working Group to expand cooperation

title: Nirmala Sitharaman inspects Bengaluru–Chennai Expressway works near Devanahalli
content: The Bengaluru–Chennai Expressway has been identified as a priority corridor under the Chennai–Bengaluru Industrial Corridor. The project originates at Hoskote near the junction of NH-4 and NH-207 in Karnataka.

title: Monsoon to advance after June 18 in Maharashtra, says Met department
content: On Saturday, the monsoon moved toward Hyderabad, coastal Odisha, parts of West Bengal, Jharkhand and Bihar

title: Four, including three children, drown as boat capsizes in the Krishna in Palnadu district; six others rescued
content: Chief Minister Chandrababu Naidu expresses grief over the loss of lives, announces ex gratia of ₹5 lakh each to the next of kin of the deceased, directs the District Collector to disburse the amount immediately

title: Kakoli claims 22 Lok Sabha MPs now in dissident TMC camp, meeting scheduled with Speaker
content: Sources in the dissident camp said a meeting of the group, initially scheduled to be held in Kolkata, has been shifted to Delhi

title: Tripura CM proposes new international flight destinations from Agartala airport
content: The proposal is aimed at tagging the MBB Airport as the international airport, citing that the new terminal building has adequate facilities to be an international airport

title: Abhishek Banerjee makes second appearance before CID in West Bengal MLAs signature forgery case
content: Trinamool spokesperson and Beleghata MLA Kunal Ghosh also arrived at around 3 p.m. and appeared before the CID in the same case

title: Senior citizens group calls for action against rising elder abuse cases
content: Awareness programmes will be held on June 15 across NTR and Krishna districts in observance of World Elder Abuse Awareness Day, aiming to identify and address issues related to the mistreatment of senior citizens

title: FORTO urges exemption from TET for legally appointed teachers
content: Forum leaders cite an NCTE clarification dated August 28, 2017, which state that NCTE has not prescribed minimum qualifications before September 3, 2001, and that State recruitment rules govern teacher appointments during that period

title: Academic initiative ‘Vijayapatham’ launched to boost student performance in KGBVs
content: The programme, launched under the slogan ‘Learning is the Goal - Success is the Destination,’ aims to secure 100% pass results, increase the number of students scoring above 590 marks, and improve overall rankings

title: Monsoon activity remains low in Andhra Pradesh due to El Niño conditions
content: The IMD-Amaravati forecasts light showers in isolated areas in three regions of South Coastal A.P. and Rayalaseema for the next week. However, thunderstorm activity is expected to continue across the State this week

title: Microbial consortia boost yield for paddy farmer
content: A paddy farmer used them on a native variety ADT-46, and the results showed an increase in yield in the first harvest in January this year

title: Monsoon tracker highlights on June 14, 2026
content: According to the Regional Meteorological Centre in Chennai, moderate rain accompanied by thunderstorm and lightning is likely to occur in northern districts of Chennai

title: Congress questions PM’s ‘silence’ on death of Indian mariners, calls Rubio’s remarks unacceptable
content: Congress asks whether PM Narendra Modi will raise the incident off Oman coast and U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio’s “threatening” and “unacceptable” statement when he meets U.S. President next week in France.

title: Remain vigilant on SIR, Meenakshi tells Congress workers
content: Urging Booth Level Agents to closely monitor voter verification, prevent wrongful deletions and help restore eligible voters’ names

title: Eight killed as vehicle falls into well in Maharashtra
content: The vehicle is being pulled out of the well, and the injured people were being rushed to the hospital, the police say

title: Irrigation dept. to restart dredging in Korapuzha
content: The dredging work was halted following mass protests in February 2026. Residents and political parties had alleged that sand was being illegally extracted under the pretext of dredging

title: School bus safety measures yet to take off despite official directives
content: The delay has drawn criticism as some school managements had earlier revised transportation charges to improve safety infrastructure, including CCTV cameras, RFID-based student facilitation systems and GPS-enabled vehicle tracking

title: Ex-BJP MP terms Ram temple funds row ‘serious’; insult to Sanatana Dharma: Akhilesh Yadav
content: Vinay Katiyar claims that some members involved in managing the trust are “thieves”; allegations a “question mark” on faith, accountability and trust, the BJP cannot “evade responsibility”, says Congress

title: Four jump off train over rumour of fire, run over by another train in Madhya Pradesh
content: Three women and a child were killed in the incident in Morena district. Their train stopped as someone pulled the alarm chain after a rumour that fire had erupted in the train spread. Railway officials say they are investigating to know why the alarm chain was pulled

title: 20 rebel Trinamool Lok Sabha MPs seek to merge with Nationalist Citizen Party of India
content: Meet Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla and announce that they are merging with the registered regional party for time being

title: The green cost: Close to 2 lakh trees likely to be felled for Bidadi AI Township
content: Pointing to the data, Nagaraju M.R., a farmer protesting against the project, questioned the government’s claims that farmers in the region are suffering losses from agriculture and that there is no demand for the crops grown there. “Which farmers who are suffering losses continue to grow such a wide variety of crops? The numbers alone suggest how farmers in this region are thriving,” he said

title: Minister Vaishnaw expects more companies to start production of memory chips in India
content: Union Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw says there is a global phenomenon of supply-demand imbalance, which is now getting addressed by setting many more data centres.

title: Odisha village to get crocodile warning system under ‘Smart Village’ plan
content: Kusunpur’s infrastructure upgrade includes a renovated water gate facility, an underground drainage system, a reconstructed community building, community toilets, and a revamped cremation centre

title: Malappuram steps up Nipah, Shigella surveillance
content: All four contacts are in the low-risk category and urged the public not to spread rumours through social media, say officials

title: Prakasam Barrage gates opened to release around 3,000 cusecs downstream
content: Water inflow has increased from local catchment area due to recent rain, and therefore, four gates have been opened between 9 a.m. and 10 a.m. to release the surplus discharge, an official statement said

title: Farmers find new customers at Nabard’s mango fest
content: They managed to sell all their produce at Mambazha Mela, an effort to bring together 16 farmer-producer organisations that produce and process mangoes

title: Modi, Macron inaugurate Innovation Conference in Nice, stress trust and cooperation in tech
content: PM Modi was warmly welcomed by the French President in Nice, where the two leaders greeted each other with a hug

title: INS Sharda concludes Colombo visit, strengthens India-Sri Lanka maritime co-operation
content: The visit featured a range of professional, training and sporting engagements between the Indian and Sri Lankan Navies

title: Disclose tender process for Board exam answer sheet procurement under RTI Act: CIC to CBSE
content: After setting aside the CBSE’s earlier denial of information under the RTI, the transparency panel asked it to furnish revised replies; the Commission found that the Central Public Information Officer had denied information under multiple exemption clauses ‘without giving any proper justification’

title: 'US-Iran peace deal now complete', declares Trump; Tehran maintains cautious tone
content: The US president also celebrated the expected resumption of maritime traffic and oil exports, posting, “Ships of the World, start your engines. Let the oil flow!” Trump's announcement came just hours before a UFC event at the White House being held to mark his 80th birthday. Just mintues before the Trump's official declaration, Pakistan PM Shehbaz Sharif had already made the agreement official.

title: 64-run hammering! Deepti and Richa sink Pakistan as India begin T20 WC with big win
content: Mandhana's 68 off 44 balls and Richa Ghosh's 34 from 17 deliveries helped India post 170 for 6 after being asked to bat. In reply, Pakistan were bowled out for 106 in 17 overs, with Muneeba Ali's 41 off 35 balls being their highest score.

title: 2x trade in 5 years, nuclear cooperation: Modi-Macron Nice meet
content: Adoption of an innovation roadmap to deepen cooperation in critical and emerging technology, launch of an economic security dialogue, another working group for AI governance and a declaration to double bilateral trade in the next five years were among the 13 outcomes announced after the meeting. Both sides also looked to ramp up defence cooperation, consistent with India’s Make in India initiative.

title: Microsoft president Brad Smith has a message for students booing tech CEOs
content: Microsoft president Brad Smith has responded to graduating students who booed AI at commencement ceremonies this spring, calling the backlash a "powerful wake-up call for the tech sector." In a 3,000-word essay, Smith acknowledged the class of 2026 faces a "perfect storm" of AI automation and tech layoffs, yet urged graduates to adapt rather than fear AI. His optimistic message arrives as Sam Altman, Mustafa Suleyman, and Dario Amodei clash over AI's job impact.

title: TMC rebels, with one MP above 2/3rd mark, eye merger with little-known regional party
content: In a dramatic development, 20 rebel Trinamool Congress MPs informed Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla that their group has merged with NCPI — which is registered in West Bengal’s Howrah and had contested a few seats in the 2023 Tripura assembly elections — and that they will be backing the BJP-led NDA. Incidentally, “to save your rights, reject political turncoats” was one of the slogans of NCPI.

title: Rescued 14 Indians from sinking dhow that suffered engine snag, says US
content: S Naval Forces Central Command/US 5th Fleet reposted a news report about the rescue operation that said “upon receiving a distress call from the stranded sailors, a US Navy P-8 aircraft reached the spot and dropped a search-and-rescue kit that included a life raft, which the 14 mariners boarded”. Official sources said that the US Navy had alerted the shore authorities and Indian Navy that a dhow with Indian sailors was sinking about 80 nautical miles east of Ras AI Hadd off the coast of Oman.

title: Modi tells India’s deep-tech story to the world, Macron backs him up
content: Before the cameras rolled at the conclave, the PM sat down with global venture capitalists and Indian entrepreneurs — OYO’s Ritesh Agarwal, Ronnie Screwvala and a clutch of international investors among them. Commerce minister Piyush Goyal and principal scientific adviser Ajay Kumar Sood were in the room. “India has emerged not as a consumer of solutions but as a contributor to solutions in the world.

title: Canada's sixth-largest state Manitoba retires student pathway to permanent residency
content: Manitoba has closed its Career Employment Pathway for international graduates, effective immediately. Candidates with active EOIs and six months of provincial work experience can now be considered under the Skilled Worker in Manitoba Pathway. This change aims to align graduates' skills with labor market needs, prioritizing those who studied and are working in the province.

title: With farmers' income in focus, Bihar govt moves to boost fisheries
content: The foundation stones will be laid by minister for fisheries and animal husbandry Rajiv Ranjan Singh and Bihar CM Samrat Choudhary. The aqua park will include carp and catfish hatcheries, Brooder incubation units, biofloc systems, a fish feed mill, water quality and disease diagnostic laboratories, involving a total investment of more than Rs 31 crore.

title: Buzz of early poll in Punjab, parties start preparations
content: The AICC’s decision to constitute a committee under Ajay Maken late Thursday night for a report on the Punjab Congress unit is said to be dictated by urgency for quick changes in the state organisation as needed. AAP is preparing for a scenario of Nov polls, and BJP is also active on Punjab front. Further, there is speculation in Congress whether Uttarakhand polls will also be brought forward.

title: 12 killed after skydiving plane crashes near Missouri airport in US
content: According to Highway Patrol Sergeant Justin Ewing, the aircraft was transporting skydivers when it went down. Emergency services received reports at around 11.30am local time that a plane had crashed and caught fire. Firefighters quickly brought the blaze under control, but Ewing described the scene as "brutal". The aircraft came down in a field next to the airport, prompting authorities to close nearby roads as a precaution.

title: Meta's highest paid employee Alexandr Wang 'admits' the company's previous AI policy didn't work
content: Meta's Chief AI Officer Alexandr Wang has admitted that the company's open-source AI playbook no longer fits its frontier models, with Muse Spark kept proprietary after early training flagged bio risk and other safety concerns. Wang says rival AI labs are seeing the same risks scale up. Meanwhile, Meta is testing subscriptions on Instagram, Facebook, WhatsApp and its AI chatbot to diversify beyond ads.

title: India restricts deployment of seafarers to conflict zones after repeated attacks in Hormuz
content: Following a fatal US military strike off the Oman coast that killed three Indian seafarers, India's Directorate General of Shipping has advised recruitment agencies to halt seafarer deployments to conflict zones. Vessels in the Gulf region are urged to maintain heightened security vigilance amid escalating risks, with crew changes permitted only in emergencies with crew consent.

title: Hidden cost of AI? Researchers warn of a slow erosion of human thinking
content: The researchers argue that as people increasingly rely on AI systems to write, analyse information and make decisions, they may become less capable of performing those tasks independently. More worryingly, they suggest that the effects may become apparent only after significant damage has already occurred. “The longer they go unaddressed, the less capacity remains to address them,” the paper warns.

title: Jude Bellingham caught up in Ashlyn Castro family drama
content: Ashlyn Castro, the girlfriend of England and Real Madrid star Jude Bellingham, is facing a public family dispute after her mother, Tina Marie Young, claimed she is living in a homeless shelter in Los Angeles. Young accused Castro of abandoning her and making false statements about her mental health. Castro has previously denied those allegations and defended her position publicly.

title: Johnny Placide questions VAR after Haiti penalty appeals rejected
content: Haiti captain Johnny Placide criticized VAR after two second-half handball appeals involving Scotland defender Grant Hanley were not reviewed during Scotland’s 1-0 Group C win in Boston. Jean-Ricner Bellegarde also questioned the decisions. Placide said Haiti respected Scotland’s victory but wanted transparency and clear explanations from match officials regarding the two controversial incidents.

title: Clavicular’s resurfaced 2025 Miami prediction missed the full story
content: An old July 2025 video of Clavicular resurfaced on X on June 14, 2026. In the clip, he talked about moving to Miami, building a business, and enjoying club life. Many people noticed that parts of that vision later became reality. The video has sparked discussion about his career, appearance changes, business ventures, and the path he has taken since then.

title: Abhishek urges Lok Sabha speaker not to recognise rebel TMC bloc
content: Ahead of TMC rebel MPs’ meeting the speaker to seek recognition as “real TMC”, MPs from party chief Mamata Banerjee’s camp Kirti Azad and Sagarika Ghosh submitted Abhishek’s letter — dated June 10, sent through email earlier — at Birla’s residence here, arguing the Constitution does not permit formation of a separate group within an existing political party.

title: To Mars with Musk: Can world's first trillionaire take you to the little red planet?
content: Elon Musk's ambitious plan to establish a permanent human settlement on Mars faces monumental challenges. SpaceX's Starship is designed for this, but orbital refuelling and survival on the harsh red planet are critical hurdles. Despite setbacks, testing progresses, with uncrewed cargo missions eyed for 2028, followed by potential human landings.

title: At 80, Trump chooses cage fight in an octagon over a rocking chair
content: Donald Trump marked his 80th birthday with a White House cage fight, a spectacle critics decried as vulgar while supporters hailed it as American exceptionalism. The event, dubbed 'UFC Freedom 250,' saw bare-knuckle combat on the South Lawn, replacing traditional presidential events.

title: Horoscope today: Astrological predictions for June 15, 2026
content: Horoscope Today: Read daily horoscope predictions for June 15, 2026. Know all about the astrological events and influences that will be affecting each of the 12 zodiac signs. Our astrologer has analyzed the movements of the planets and the alignment of the stars to bring you the most accurate and up-to-date horoscope predictions for the day ahead. Get ready to discover what the stars have in store for you today.

title: History at Edgbaston! India-Pakistan shatters Women's T20 World Cup record
content: A record-breaking crowd of 18,814 witnessed India's dominant victory over Pakistan at Edgbaston, setting a new attendance record for a Women's T20 World Cup group-stage match. Smriti Mandhana's 68 and Deepti Sharma's career-best 5/10 propelled India to a commanding 64-run win, highlighting the growing popularity of women's cricket and the India-Pakistan rivalry.

title: Battle of real ‘TMC’ begins as rebel MPs merge with Nationalist Citizens Party
content: A faction of Trinamool Congress MPs claims to have broken away and merged with the Nationalist Citizens Party, seeking separate recognition in Parliament. The Mamata Banerjee-led TMC faction disputes this, asserting their claim to the party's identity and electoral symbol, with the matter likely heading to court.

title: Watch: Thousands of British Kashmiris protest outside UK Parliament over PoK unrest
content: British Kashmiris protested in London against alleged human rights abuses and excessive force by Pakistani security forces in Pakistan-occupied Jammu and Kashmir. Demonstrators highlighted civilian deaths, injuries, and harassment, demanding international intervention and accountability for the ongoing unrest driven by economic grievances and governance issues.

title: ‘Armed forces have full freedom’: Rajnath Singh says India knows how to ensure peace
content: Defence Minister Rajnath Singh declared India's readiness to decisively counter security threats, granting armed forces full autonomy against terrorism. He criticized past governments' soft approach, highlighting current freedom to eliminate terrorists, even across borders. Singh also lauded Maharana Pratap as a symbol of national pride and linked his values to the Modi government's governance philosophy.

title: Swiss referendum fails as voters block '10 million' population cap plan
content: Swiss voters have rejected a right-wing proposal to cap the nation's population at 10 million. Early results show a clear opposition to the initiative, which was championed by the Swiss People's Party over concerns about immigration and its impact on infrastructure and resources. The measure's defeat preserves Switzerland's crucial ties with the European Union.

title: Rope jumping accident: Woman, 21, dies after safety rope mishap in Brazil
content: A tragic accident claimed the life of a 21-year-old Brazilian woman during a rope jump in São Paulo. Staff reportedly failed to attach her safety rope before launching her from a 40-meter bridge. Shocking footage captured the moments before her fatal fall, showing the rope still on the platform as she plummeted into a rocky canyon.

title: ‘Bring back mortal remains’: Wife of Indian seafarer who died aboard vessel in Oman
content: A grieving family is desperately seeking the repatriation of Indian seafarer Nishanth Uirthanathan's body from Oman and a thorough investigation into his death aboard MT Celestial. They allege a severe lack of communication from the shipping company and inadequate medical care. The Indian Embassy in Muscat is coordinating efforts for the body's return.

title: 20 colleagues, liquor, party: Hyderabad techie found dead in pool after argument
content: A software employee's death under suspicious circumstances at a Telangana farmhouse has sparked a police investigation. Gundla Madhu, 27, was discovered at a company get-together in Yadadri Bhuvanagiri district. His father filed a complaint, leading to a registered case. Authorities are questioning attendees to unravel the events.

title: Connolly's maiden ODI ton helps Australia avoid Bangladesh whitewash
content: Australia secured a thrilling one-wicket win against Bangladesh in the final ODI. Cooper Connolly starred with a career-best 149 runs. He anchored the chase as Australia pursued 275. Shoriful Islam took six wickets for Bangladesh. Australia reached the target with three balls remaining. This prevented a series whitewash for the visitors.

title: Re-NEET admit card released for exam on June 21: Direct link to download hall tickets here
content: The National Testing Agency has released NEET UG 2026 re-examination admit cards on neet.nta.nic.in. Candidates can download their hall tickets using application number and date of birth. The re-exam is scheduled for June 21, 2026, for approximately 22.75 lakh registered candidates. Candidates can check the steps provided here to download the Re-NEET admit cards from the official website.

title: US-Iran War LIVE Updates: Trump Slams Israel Attack On Lebanon As Iran Sees 'No Point' Of US Talks
content: US-Iran War LIVE Updates: Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesperson Esmaeil Baqaei reportedly denied that a memorandum of understanding would be signed.

title: All About Nationalist Citizens Party, New Home Of 20 Rebel Trinamool MPs
content: The NCPI was registered in Bengal in January 2023. Records show it got donations totalling Rs 1.13 lakh. Shewly Kundu is mentioned as treasurer of the party whose registered address is in Bengal's Howrah

title: After Rebel Bloc's Big Merger Announcement, Trinamool's "Betrayal" Jab
content: "They (the rebels) had contested the election under the Trinamool symbol, saying they want to strengthen the party under Mamata Banerjee's leadership. Now they have moved away from the promise. That's deceit," Madan Mitra said.

title: New Rajkot International Airport Boosts Industrial Growth, Property Price
content: As the airport is close to Hirasar and Kuwadva GIDC, local industries such as engineering, art jewellery, and Shapar-Veraval industries have benefited greatly.

title: Locked In, Beaten, Forced To Work, Bengal Woman Rescued In Gurugram
content: A 39-year-old Adivasi woman from West Bengal's Birbhum district was rescued from alleged bonded labour in Gurugram on Friday, after a chance phone call to her family triggered a coordinated operation involving police, district authorities and civil s

title: More Than 73,000 People Have Died Till Now Due To Israel's Attacks On Gaza
content: Nearly 1,000 people have been killed in Gaza since the start of the ceasefire, according to Gaza's health ministry. Five Israeli soldiers have been killed since the truce.

title: "No F*cking Judgement": Trump Fumes At Netanyahu As Israel Strikes Lebanon
content: Trump said the US-Iran peace deal that will potentially end the war and open the Strait of Hormuz was still on track despite an Iranian threat to retaliate.

title: Bengal MPs Think Long Term, Next Big Move In July, Hints Newest Rebel
content: "When you leave with two-thirds of the party, you cannot demand the name of that party on the first day... In July, we will make a demand to give us the Trinamool (name)," Sudip Bandopadhyay said.

title: 8 Killed As Pick-Up Vehicle Falls In Well In Maharashtra: Cops
content: Eight people, including four women and four children, were killed while seven others were injured on Sunday when a pick-up vehicle carrying them plunged into a roadside well in Malshiras tehsil in Solapur district, police said.

title: 3 Killed, 29 Injured As Truck Rams Bus In Madhya Pradesh: Cops
content: An out-of-control truck rammed into a car, hit a bus and crashed into a bridge in Katni, Madhya Pradesh, on Sunday, leaving three passengers in the bus dead and 29 injured, police said.

title: On Road To Mamata Banerjee's Downfall, What The Signposts Say
content: The Trinamool Congress, which had a massive strength of 215 MLAs until early May, has shrunk to just 20 MLAs within 40 days of the results

title: Gujarat Man Who Conned Women Through Fake Matrimonial Profiles Arrested
content: To quickly build emotional intimacy and lower his victims' guard, he falsely claimed to be a childless widower living a lonely life.

title: Video: Helicopters Collide Mid-Air In Rio De Janeiro, 6 Crew Members Dead
content: Images of the scene in local media showed a thick plume of black smoke rising from the car dealership, where several cars were on fire.

title: Iran Slams US After Israel Attacks Lebanon, Says "No Point" In Peace Talks
content: The latest hurdle came hours after Israel said its military had carried out strikes targeting Iran-backed Hezbollah in Beirut's southern suburbs.

title: Trump Hosts 'Freedom 250' Cage Fights On 80th Birthday Amid War, Inflation
content: Trump is set to watch 14 mixed martial arts fighters from five countries try to kick, punch and grapple their way to a victory before an expected audience of 4,000 people.

title: Farmers Who Gave Land For Noida Airport To Board 1st Flight Tomorrow
content: Situated in Gautam Buddh Nagar district under the Yamuna Expressway Industrial Development Authority, the airport is among the Uttar Pradesh government's marquee infrastructure projects

title: Sunday bloody Sunday: Trump warns Israel not to blow up Iran deal
content: "this morning's attack on Beirut should not have happened," Trump says . "this could be the beginning of a long and beautiful peace... Let's not blow it!" the president urges restraint after an Israeli strike on beirut threatens to derail a peace deal .

title: BSF ASI shoots himself dead at Jammu camp
content:
Representative image

JAMMU: A BSF assistant sub-inspector (ASI) allegedly shot himself dead at his camp in Jammu on Sunday, a day after resuming his duty following a month-long leave. “ASI Lal Singh (54), from Madhya Pradesh, was found in a pool of blood... Senior BSF and police officials rushed to the scene... An inquest has been initiated to ascertain the circumstances behind his taking such an extreme step," an official said.

End of Article
